name: Gregory Castellanos
recommendation: Mukta Sambrani, a former education colleague, recommends Greg for his technical expertise, creative energy, event management, art and technology education, and multimedia work.
description: The umbrella site for Gregory Castellanos, a Northern California founder, learning designer, executive function coach, AI evaluation specialist, event producer, and inventor.

areas.name: People
areas.purpose: Executive function, practical independence, education, and support for neurodivergent teens, adults, and families.
areas.intro: Executive-function coaching since 2020 for Autistic and ADHD clients, including Regional Center and Self-Determination Program participants.

areas.name: Ideas
areas.purpose: Strategy and project development for ambitious ideas, unusual opportunities, and complicated plans.
areas.intro: Strategy and project development for ambitious ideas, unusual opportunities, and complicated plans.

areas.name: Brands
areas.purpose: Brand strategy, messaging, websites, presentations, identity direction, and creative support.
areas.intro: Brand strategy, messaging, websites, presentations, identity direction, and creative support.

areas.name: Technology
areas.purpose: Human-centered AI, digital tools, creative technology, education, and responsible adoption.
areas.intro: AI literacy, rubric-based evaluation, creative technology, and responsible adoption.

areas.name: Experiences
areas.purpose: Events, retreats, sound, production, and community gatherings shaped with intention.
areas.intro: Events, retreats, sound, production, and community gatherings shaped with intention.

areas.name: Places
areas.purpose: Creative property stewardship, beautification, hospitality, guest readiness, and thoughtful activation.
areas.intro: Creative property stewardship, beautification, hospitality, guest readiness, and thoughtful activation.

work.name: Castellanos Coaching
work.overview: Executive-function coaching for Autistic and ADHD clients, including Regional Center and Self-Determination Program participants.
work.situation: Neurodivergent teens and adults often need person-centered systems that connect goals, daily life, and real readiness.
work.contribution: Served 30+ clients cumulatively since 2020, building person-centered plans and individualized progress tracking around college, employment, and independent-living readiness.

work.name: Local Maker Mart
work.overview: A nonprofit arts and maker hub in North Beach, San Francisco, built to connect participation, commerce, learning, and community in one physical place.
work.situation: Creative local projects need systems that make participation, commerce, learning, and community feel connected.
work.contribution: Owned facility acquisition, nonprofit governance, grant writing, partnerships, youth STEAM programming, and community-facing events that engaged hundreds of artists and makers.
work.status: Legacy venture and reference point for current community-building work.

work.name: Recreation Sound Systems
work.overview: An original sound, product-invention, and event-production venture connecting audio design, product development, entrepreneurship, branding, technical production, and gatherings.
work.situation: Events need more than gear. They need calm, preparation, good taste, and technical systems that support the human experience.
work.role: Founder, inventor, producer, and technical director, 2013-2020
work.contribution: The work produced two U.S. design patents: a portable sound system and a portable yoga block speaker.

work.name: TechShop STEAM and Maker Education
work.overview: Hands-on STEAM curriculum and project-based learning built around fabrication, technology, design, creativity, and practical problem solving.
work.situation: Hands-on learning works best when tools, teaching, confidence, and real projects are brought into one understandable sequence.
work.role: Curriculum designer, educator, and program developer, 2014-2019
work.contribution: Taught hundreds of learners, designed project-based sequences across 2D/3D design, laser cutting, CNC, 3D printing, and electronics, and co-designed TechShop Inside, a mobile makerspace for underserved communities.
work.privacy: Publicly described at a high level

work.name: Enterprise Event Production
work.overview: More than fifteen years of work across live sound, video, playback, show calling, technical direction, and complex event operations.
work.situation: Complex events need calm technical judgment, clear communication, and production systems that support the room.
work.role: Technical director, audio and video professional, show caller, producer, and production partner
work.contribution: Supported productions for Google, Stripe, Atlassian, GitHub, Nasdaq, SF MoMA, and the World Economic Forum through these production companies; manages 200+ slide decks per show and live cue-to-cue workflows.
work.status: Selected production experience, described without implying direct client relationships.

work.name: Shangri-La Sonoma
work.overview: A Sonoma retreat center where Greg has supported event production, web design, guest-experience thinking, and place-readiness.
work.situation: A property is a set of experiences: where people gather, what they hear, what they see after dark, and what everyone has gotten used to overlooking.
work.contribution: Produced events including Ghost Town Gala 2025 and contributed to hospitality, production, presentation, and guest-experience systems.
work.status: Selected property work, with ownership and operations details intentionally not implied.
work.privacy: Private ownership and operational details withheld

work.name: Convergence 2026
work.overview: A sold-out 250-person, 15-artist production shaped through budget, stage management, and run-of-show leadership.
work.situation: Multi-artist productions need a clear container, calm operations, and a run-of-show that lets the room move well.
work.contribution: Owned budget, stage management, and run-of-show for a sold-out 250-person production with 15 artists.

work.name: Stay In The Light
work.overview: A retreat and gathering concept shaped around presence, hospitality, and place-based experience.
work.situation: A meaningful retreat needs a clear point of view, a trusted container, and careful attention to setting.
work.contribution: Connected idea, language, environment, flow, hospitality, and production planning into a coherent concept.
work.status: Concept and project material, with private location details protected.

work.name: Kolob Gate Gardens
work.overview: A place-based garden and hospitality concept connected to stewardship, beauty, and gathering.
work.situation: A place with promise needs patient care, a clear point of view, and practical activation.
work.contribution: Connects place care, hospitality, programming, and guest-readiness thinking.
work.status: Selected project with private operational details withheld.

current: AI literacy and structured evaluation for schools, teams, and creative projects.
Executive function coaching and practical independence systems through Castellanos Coaching.
Events, learning programs, and Make Places Better projects that help places come alive.
